name: 4 Vesta
description: The second-most-massive asteroid and the brightest one visible from Earth. NASA's Dawn spacecraft orbited it in 2011–2012 and found a world with a giant impact basin at its south pole.
facts: Around 1 in 20 meteorites found on Earth are chips knocked off Vesta.

name: 2 Pallas
description: The third-largest asteroid, on an unusually steep orbit tilted almost 35° out of the plane of the planets.
facts: Its extreme tilt makes Pallas one of the hardest large asteroids for spacecraft to visit.

name: 10 Hygiea
description: The fourth-largest object in the asteroid belt, a dark carbonaceous body that is nearly spherical — some astronomers argue it qualifies as a dwarf planet.

name: 433 Eros
description: A near-Earth asteroid shaped like a peanut, and the first asteroid ever orbited and landed on by a spacecraft (NEAR Shoemaker, 2001).
facts: Eros crosses the orbit of Mars and comes within 0.15 AU of Earth's orbit.
